🗺️ Cheapest Months to Fly to Europe

2026-03-07
Europe
flights
timing
budget

European flight prices can vary 300% depending on when you fly. July flights to Paris can cost $900 while February flights cost $300. Here's the complete month-by-month breakdown for 2026.

📊 European Flight Prices by Month (US East Coast)

  • January — $300-500 (CHEAPEST, except New Year's week)

  • February — $300-450 (VERY CHEAP, cold but great deals)

  • March — $400-600 (prices rising, Easter spike)

  • April — $500-700 (spring starts, popular)

  • May — $600-800 (beautiful weather, high demand)

  • June — $700-1000 (summer begins, expensive)

  • July — $800-1200 (PEAK, most expensive)

  • August — $800-1100 (still peak, slightly less than July)

  • September — $550-750 (shoulder season, great value)

  • October — $500-700 (excellent weather, good prices)

  • November — $350-550 (off-season, bargains)

  • December — $400-900 (cheap early month, spikes for Christmas)

🏆 Best Months to Fly to Europe

  • #1: February — cheapest, fewer tourists, winter charm

  • #2: November — great prices, Christmas markets starting

  • #3: January (after New Year) — dead cheap, cold but cozy

  • #4: Early September — still warm, prices drop after Labor Day

  • #5: Late October — fall colors, low prices

❌ Worst Months to Fly to Europe

  • #1: July — peak summer, highest prices, huge crowds

  • #2: August — still peak, Europeans on vacation too

  • #3: June — summer starts, school's out, pricey

  • #4: Christmas week (Dec 20-Jan 2) — sky-high prices

  • #5: Easter week — unpredictable dates, always expensive

🌍 Best Months by Destination

  • London — Jan-Feb (cold but cheap), Sep-Oct (pleasant)

  • Paris — Feb-Mar, Nov (avoid Jun-Aug)

  • Rome — Nov-Feb (mild winter), Apr-May shoulder

  • Barcelona — Nov-Mar (still nice weather)

  • Amsterdam — Feb-Mar, Nov (tulips in April = pricey)

  • Greek Islands — May or Oct (summer = expensive)

  • Iceland — Feb-Mar (Northern Lights), Sep (still accessible)

Search cheap flights to London

💰 How Much You Save

  • February vs July to Paris: save $400-600 per ticket

  • November vs June to Rome: save $300-500 per ticket

  • January vs August to London: save $350-550 per ticket

  • For a couple: $800-1200 savings by traveling off-season

  • For a family of 4: $1600-2400 savings

🌦️ Weather Trade-offs

Yes, winter is colder. But: London in February is 40-50°F (not that cold), Paris in November is pleasant (50-55°F), and Rome in January is mild (50-60°F). Pack a jacket, save $500, avoid tourist crowds — worth it.

💡 Pro Tips

  • Book 3-4 months ahead even for cheap months

  • Avoid school holiday weeks (spring break, Thanksgiving, Christmas)

  • First/last week of month often cheaper than middle weeks

  • Mix destinations — combine cheap month in one city with another

  • Tuesday/Wednesday flights save extra 15-20% even in cheap months

Bottom line: If you can choose when to visit Europe, go in January-March or September-November. You'll save 40-60% on flights, hotels will be cheaper too, and you'll actually enjoy cities without the summer tourist madness.
